mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate compresses the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which increases equity present values and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of the lowest-quintile FEDFUNDS observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals that labor market deterioration has peaked and a recovery is underway, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations stabilize over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.1 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances following a month-over-month UNRATE decline greater than 0.1 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment indicate coordinated demand and labor market expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of cases, or the joint signal does not outperform the better of the two single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices during a zero-interest-rate period erode real corporate margins and signal future monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation pressure
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns follow month-over-month INDPRO increases in fewer than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 550 thousand units and rising for two consecutive months signal a revival in residential investment and construction employment, lifting cyclical equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 550 and rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST is above 550 and has risen for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production concurrent with a month-over-month rise in retail sales signals a supply-demand imbalance that compresses corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month while RSAFS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines month-over-month while RSAFS rises month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.
